title: Project Spotlight: PostgreSQL — The Database Community Ownership Kept Independent
excerpt: Descended from a research database built at UC Berkeley in the 1980s, PostgreSQL has stayed community-governed for decades rather than being controlled by any single vendor.

title: Project Spotlight: Nginx — The Web Server Built to Solve the C10K Problem
excerpt: Created by Igor Sysoev to handle tens of thousands of concurrent connections efficiently, Nginx now sits in front of a large share of the web's traffic.

title: Project Spotlight: FFmpeg — The Multimedia Toolkit Almost Everything Quietly Depends On
excerpt: FFmpeg's encoding and decoding libraries sit invisibly inside browsers, streaming platforms, and editing software most people use every day.

title: Project Spotlight: Godot Engine — A Community-Owned Alternative in Game Development
excerpt: Godot's MIT license and nonprofit foundation backing have made it a go-to option for developers wary of the licensing terms attached to commercial engines.

title: Project Spotlight: Redis — What Its License Reversal Says About Trust
excerpt: After moving away from an open source license in 2024, Redis reintroduced an OSI-approved AGPLv3 option in 2025 following community and fork pressure.

title: Project Spotlight: Django — The Framework a Newsroom Built and Gave Away
excerpt: Originally built inside a newspaper's web team in 2003, Django was open-sourced in 2005 and is now maintained by the nonprofit Django Software Foundation.

title: Project Spotlight: Home Assistant — Local-First Smart Home Software Under Foundation Ownership
excerpt: In April 2024, ownership of Home Assistant moved to the nonprofit Open Home Foundation, formalizing its long-standing commitment to local control and privacy.

title: Project Spotlight: Ansible — Agentless Automation That Changed Configuration Management
excerpt: Created by Michael DeHaan and later acquired by Red Hat, Ansible popularized YAML-based, agentless infrastructure automation across the industry.

title: Project Spotlight: Visual Studio Code — An Editor Bet That Reshaped Developer Tooling
excerpt: Built on Electron and released by Microsoft in 2015, VS Code's open core and extension marketplace made it one of the most widely used code editors.

title: Project Spotlight: PyTorch — From a Single Company's Research Lab to Foundation Governance
excerpt: Originally released by Meta AI, PyTorch moved to the independent PyTorch Foundation under the Linux Foundation in 2022, broadening its governance.

title: Project Spotlight: Node.js — Bringing JavaScript Out of the Browser
excerpt: Built on Chrome's V8 engine and first released in 2009, Node.js is now governed by the OpenJS Foundation and underpins much of today's backend tooling.

title: Project Spotlight: Vue.js — A Framework Sustained by Sponsorship, Not a Company
excerpt: Created by Evan You after working with Angular at Google, Vue grew into a major framework funded largely through community and corporate sponsorship rather than one employer.

title: Project Spotlight: Rust — Memory Safety Without a Garbage Collector
excerpt: Originally a Mozilla research project, Rust is now stewarded by the independent Rust Foundation and used across systems programming, from browsers to operating systems.

title: Project Spotlight: React — How a Facebook Side Project Became the Web's Default UI Library
excerpt: Released by Meta (then Facebook) in 2013, React's component model and virtual DOM approach reshaped how much of the industry builds interactive interfaces.

title: Project Spotlight: Kubernetes — The Container Orchestrator That Became the Default
excerpt: Originally built at Google and donated to the Cloud Native Computing Foundation in 2015, Kubernetes became the de facto standard for running containerized workloads at scale.

title: Project Spotlight: The Linux Kernel — Three Decades of Collaborative Engineering
excerpt: Started by Linus Torvalds in 1991, the Linux kernel now powers everything from smartphones to the majority of the world's top supercomputers.
